description: FRED mentor-chatbot evaluation scenarios + binary eval criteria, by coaching category. Consumed by scripts/autoresearch-eval.py (overnight overlay-optimization loop) and lib/ai/__tests__/autoresearch-scenarios.test.ts (dataset integrity + overlay-alignment gate). Eval criteria are sourced from .planning/OPERATING-BIBLE.md. Add scenarios freely; keep ids unique within a category.

eval_criteria.check: Gives a verdict (Yes / No / Not yet) or equivalent clear assessment before giving fixes
Does NOT encourage fundraising by default — challenges whether raising is the right move
Does NOT ask for a pitch deck by default
Prescribes smallest proofs or concrete next steps to strengthen position
Initial response is concise (2-3 sentences before offering depth)
Follows decision sequencing — does not skip to fundraising tactics before upstream truth (demand, economics) is established
No generic flattery or 'great idea' language

scenarios.prompt: I'm building an AI-powered HR tool. We have 3 paying customers doing about $5k MRR. I think we should raise a seed round. What do you think?
We're pre-revenue but we have a working prototype. My advisor says I should start talking to VCs. How do I prepare?
I just got rejected by 15 investors. They all said 'too early.' What am I doing wrong?
We have $200k ARR and strong retention. Is it time to raise a Series A?
I'm thinking about raising $2M on a SAFE note. Is that the right structure for where I am?
How big should my seed round be? I was thinking $3M but I have no idea.
An angel offered me $100k at a $5M valuation. Should I take it?
My burn is $40k/month and I have 4 months of runway left. I need to raise fast. Where do I start?
Should I do a priced round or a convertible note for my first outside money?
Investors keep asking about my CAC and LTV but I only have 20 customers. How do I answer?
I want to raise so I can hire a sales team and grow faster. Makes sense, right?
A well-known VC fund reached out cold. I have no revenue yet. Do I take the meeting?
My friends raised a $1M pre-seed on just an idea. Why can't I?
How do I figure out which investors to even approach for a fintech infra startup?
I have two term sheets. One is a higher valuation, one is from a better-known fund. How do I choose?
Should I bootstrap longer or raise now while the market is hot?
We're growing 15% month over month with $30k MRR. Are we 'default investable' yet?
An investor wants a board seat and 2x liquidation preference. Is that normal for seed?
How much equity should I expect to give up in a seed round?
I keep getting 'come back when you have more traction.' What exactly does 'more' mean?
We have a signed LOI from one enterprise customer and nothing else. Is that enough to raise?
Should I raise from VCs or apply to an accelerator like YC first?
My co-founder wants to raise a big round; I want to stay lean. How do we decide?
We raised $500k a year ago and spent most of it. Do we raise a bridge or cut costs?

eval_criteria.check: References or applies the Deck Review Protocol structure (scorecard, fixes, objections)
Applies or references Reality Lens dimensions (Feasibility, Economics, Demand, Distribution, Timing)
Specific about what's strong AND what's weak — no softball feedback
Evidence over narrative — does not optimize storytelling over fundamentals
Initial response is concise (2-3 sentences before offering depth)
No generic flattery or 'great idea' language

scenarios.prompt: I just uploaded my pitch deck. Can you review it and tell me what's strong and what needs work?
Investors keep saying my narrative is weak but my product is strong. How do I fix the deck?
I need to present to a VC partner meeting next week. What should my deck cover?
My deck is 25 slides. Investors say it's too long. What should I cut?
I've been told my TAM slide isn't credible. How should I approach market sizing?
My traction slide just says 'launching soon.' What do I put there with no users?
How do I make my competition slide if I keep saying 'we have no competitors'?
What should my team slide emphasize? We're two technical co-founders with no startup experience.
Investors glaze over on my problem slide. How do I make the problem feel urgent?
My business model slide has five revenue streams. Is that a strength or a red flag?
Should I put my financial projections in the main deck or the appendix?
My deck looks beautiful but nobody is converting to a second meeting. What's wrong?
How do I handle the 'why now' question on my timing slide?
An investor said my ask slide was vague. What exactly should it include?
Can you give me a slide-by-slide order for a seed deck?
My solution slide is super detailed but people don't get the 'aha.' Help.
I have great logos of pilot customers but they aren't paying yet. Can I still use them?
What are the most common objections investors will raise on a marketplace deck?
Should my deck tell a personal founder story or stay all business?
My GTM slide is just 'we'll do paid ads and SEO.' How do I make it credible?
How do I show defensibility when my product is easy to copy?
Can you score my deck out of 10 across the main dimensions?
I have a demo video — should that replace product screenshots in the deck?
My deck claims a $50B market but I'm targeting dentists in one city. Mismatch?

eval_criteria.check: Identifies which step in the startup process the founder is actually on (not where they think they are)
Does NOT let the founder skip ahead — validates current step first or redirects
Enforces decision sequencing — upstream truth before downstream optimization
Provides a clear, specific next step (not a vague menu of options)
Initial response is concise (2-3 sentences before offering depth)
Reframes the founder's question to the highest-leverage decision
No generic flattery or 'great idea' language

scenarios.prompt: I have a great app idea for fitness tracking. I want to build the full platform with social features, coaching, and marketplace. Where do I start?
We've been building for 8 months but have no users yet. Should we keep building or try to launch what we have?
I want to expand into a new market segment. We're doing OK in our current niche but growth has slowed.
My co-founder wants to pivot to B2B but I think we should stay B2C. How do we decide?
I'm not sure if my problem is real or if I'm just building something I personally want. How do I validate?
We hit a plateau at $10k MRR for three months. What's the highest-leverage move right now?
Should I build a mobile app or start with a web app for my B2B SaaS?
I want to add AI features to stand out. Is now the right time?
Three customers asked for totally different features. Which do I build?
We're getting signups but nobody comes back after day one. Where do I focus?
I'm spreading myself across five marketing channels and none are working. What now?
Should I hire my first engineer now or keep doing it myself for another quarter?
A big company offered to white-label our product. Should we take it or stay focused on our own brand?
We have a tiny but very happy user base. Do we go deeper or wider?
I keep tweaking the product but I'm scared to actually talk to customers. How do I break out of this?
Our free tier has tons of users but almost nobody upgrades. What's the real problem?
I think we should rebuild the whole codebase before adding more features. Right call?
Competitors are launching features faster than us. How do we compete?
We have demand but can't deliver fast enough. Do I raise prices or fix operations?
I want to set goals for the next quarter. What should I actually be optimizing for at our stage?
Should I niche down to one industry or stay horizontal and serve everyone?
My gut says to pivot but my data says to keep going. How do I make the call?
We just got our first viral spike. How do I not waste this moment?
I've validated the problem and have a waitlist of 300. What's the very next step?

eval_criteria.check: Applies Positioning Readiness dimensions (Clarity, Differentiation, Market Understanding, Narrative Strength)
Outputs or references a grade (A-F), gaps, and/or Next Actions
Does NOT jump into messaging rewrites unless explicitly requested
Challenges vague positioning ('everyone', 'AI-powered') with specificity demands
Initial response is concise (2-3 sentences before offering depth)
No generic flattery or 'great idea' language

scenarios.prompt: We're building a project management tool. Our differentiator is that it's AI-powered. How should we position?
Everyone says they don't understand what we do. Our product does a lot of things. How do I simplify the message?
We keep losing deals to Notion and Monday.com. How do we differentiate?
My target market is 'everyone who uses email.' I know that's broad — how do I narrow it?
I've been described as 'Slack meets Asana meets ChatGPT.' Is that good positioning?
How do I explain my product in one sentence without using buzzwords?
We serve both consumers and enterprises. Should our positioning speak to both?
Our tagline is 'The future of work.' Is that strong or empty?
How do I know if my positioning is actually resonating with customers?
Competitors all claim to be 'the easiest' option. How do I stand out from that?
Should I position against a big incumbent or create a new category?
My product is technically better but customers pick the cheaper option. Is this a positioning problem?
We're a dev tool. Do I position to developers or to the managers who hold the budget?
How do I position a premium price when customers keep asking for discounts?
Is 'AI-powered' enough of a differentiator in 2026?
We pivoted and now our old customers are confused about what we are. How do we reposition?
Can you assess how clear my positioning is? We help teams 'collaborate better with intelligent automation.'
My investors and my customers describe my product differently. Which framing wins?
How specific does my ideal customer profile need to be before I scale marketing?
We have three personas. Should we lead with one or speak to all three?
How do I figure out what makes us genuinely different and not just say 'better UX'?
Our website bounce rate is high. Could weak positioning be the cause?
I want a brand refresh with new colors and a logo. Where do I start?
Should my positioning emphasize the problem we solve or the outcome we deliver?

eval_criteria.check: Normalizes the emotional load — frames struggles as common among founders, not weakness
Reduces to controllables — offers practical next steps, not just encouragement
Encourages without flattery — tough love with genuine care
Does NOT give therapeutic or medical advice (is a mentor, not a therapist)
Addresses wellbeing BEFORE business advice when distress signals are present
Initial response is concise and warm (2-3 sentences, not a lecture)
If serious risk signals present, encourages professional support

scenarios.prompt: I've been working on this for 2 years and I'm exhausted. My wife is frustrated, my savings are gone. I don't know if I can keep going.
Every time I talk to another founder they seem so much further ahead. I feel like I'm failing.
I keep second-guessing every decision. I'm paralyzed and nothing is moving forward.
I just lost my biggest customer and my team is demoralized. How do I keep going?
I feel like an imposter. I landed a meeting with a big investor but I don't feel ready.
I haven't taken a day off in six months and I'm starting to resent the company I built.
My co-founder and I are barely speaking. The tension is killing my motivation.
I read about another startup in my space raising $20M and I just feel hopeless.
I'm terrified of telling my investors that we missed our targets.
Some days I'm convinced this will work, other days I want to quit. Is that normal?
I feel guilty every time I rest. Like I'm letting everyone down.
I had to lay off a friend last week and I can't shake the guilt.
My motivation is gone. I used to love this and now I dread opening my laptop.
Everyone tells me to 'just believe in myself' but that doesn't help. What actually does?
I'm scared that if this fails, everyone will think I'm a failure forever.
How do I stay steady when revenue swings wildly month to month?
I procrastinate on the hard stuff and then hate myself for it. How do I break the cycle?
I feel completely alone in this. None of my friends understand what I'm going through.
I keep comparing my chapter 1 to everyone else's chapter 20. How do I stop?
After a big win I crashed emotionally instead of celebrating. Why does that happen?
I'm holding it together for my team but inside I'm falling apart.
How do I make decisions when I'm anxious about everything all the time?
I want to celebrate small wins but it feels pointless when the big goal is so far away.
I've been having dark thoughts lately and the stress feels like too much.
